types.name: Point2d
types.doc: Point2d is a 2D point with float64 coordinates, mirroring cv::Point2d.

funcs.name: Centroid2d
funcs.doc:
Centroid2d returns the arithmetic mean of the points. It panics on an empty
slice.

funcs.name: Lerp2d
funcs.signature: func Lerp2d(a, b Point2d, t float64) Point2d
funcs.doc: Lerp2d linearly interpolates from a to b by parameter t.

funcs.name: Pt2d
funcs.doc: Pt2d builds a Point2d from x and y.

funcs.name: RotatePoint2d
funcs.signature: func RotatePoint2d(p, center Point2d, angleDeg float64) Point2d
funcs.doc:
RotatePoint2d rotates p about center by angleDeg degrees clockwise in image
coordinates (y down) and returns the result.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the vector sum p+o.

methods.name: Cross
methods.doc: Cross returns the 2D cross product (z-component) p×o.

methods.name: Ddot
methods.doc: Ddot is an alias for Dot kept for symmetry with the integer types.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot product p·o.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether p and o are the same point.

methods.name: Inside
methods.doc: Inside reports whether the point lies within rectangle r.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the point scaled by s.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean length of the position vector.

methods.name: Round
methods.doc: Round returns the nearest integer point.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the point as (x, y).

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the vector difference p-o.

types.name: Point2f
types.doc: Point2f is a 2D point with float32 coordinates, mirroring cv::Point2f.

funcs.name: Centroid2f
funcs.doc:
Centroid2f returns the arithmetic mean of the points. It panics on an empty
slice.

funcs.name: ConvexHull2f
funcs.doc:
ConvexHull2f computes the convex hull of the points with Andrew's monotone
chain algorithm and returns the hull vertices in counter-clockwise order (in
a y-up frame). Fewer than three input points are returned deduplicated.

funcs.name: Lerp2f
funcs.signature: func Lerp2f(a, b Point2f, t float64) Point2f
funcs.doc: Lerp2f linearly interpolates from a to b by parameter t (0 gives a, 1 gives b).

funcs.name: MidPoint2f
funcs.doc: MidPoint2f returns the midpoint of segment a-b.

funcs.name: Pt2f
funcs.doc: Pt2f builds a Point2f from x and y.

funcs.name: RotatePoint2f
funcs.signature: func RotatePoint2f(p, center Point2f, angleDeg float64) Point2f
funcs.doc:
RotatePoint2f rotates p about center by angleDeg degrees clockwise in image
coordinates (y down) and returns the result.

funcs.name: SegmentsIntersect2f
funcs.signature: func SegmentsIntersect2f(p1, p2, p3, p4 Point2f) (Point2f, bool)
funcs.doc:
SegmentsIntersect2f reports whether segments p1-p2 and p3-p4 cross and, when
they do, returns their intersection point. Collinear overlap returns false.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the vector sum p+o.

methods.name: Cross
methods.doc: Cross returns the 2D cross product (z-component) p×o.

methods.name: Ddot
methods.doc: Ddot returns the dot product accumulated in float64.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot product p·o.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether p and o are the same point.

methods.name: Inside
methods.doc: Inside reports whether the point lies within rectangle r.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the point scaled by s.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean length of the position vector.

methods.name: Round
methods.doc: Round returns the nearest integer point.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the point as (x, y).

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the vector difference p-o.

methods.name: ToPoint2d
methods.doc: ToPoint2d converts to a float64 point.

types.name: Point2i
types.doc:
Point2i is a 2D point with integer coordinates, mirroring cv::Point2i
(a.k.a. cv::Point). X is the column and Y is the row.

funcs.name: Clamp2i
funcs.signature: func Clamp2i(p Point2i, r Rect2i) Point2i
funcs.doc:
Clamp2i clamps point p to lie within rectangle r (the returned point is on
the closed range [X, X+Width-1] x [Y, Y+Height-1]).

funcs.name: Pt2i
funcs.doc: Pt2i builds a Point2i from x and y.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the vector sum p+o.

methods.name: Cross
methods.doc: Cross returns the 2D cross product (z-component) p×o.

methods.name: Ddot
methods.doc: Ddot returns the dot product accumulated in float64.

methods.name: Dot

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether p and o are the same point.

methods.name: Inside
methods.doc:
Inside reports whether the point lies within rectangle r (edges inclusive on
the top-left, exclusive on the bottom-right, matching OpenCV).

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the point scaled by s.

methods.name: Norm

methods.name: String

methods.doc: ToPoint2f converts to a float32 point.

types.name: Point3d

funcs.name: Pt3d
funcs.signature: func Pt3d(x, y, z float64) Point3d

methods.name: Add

funcs.doc: Pt3i builds a Point3i from x, y and z.

types.name: Quatd
types.doc:
Quatd is a double-precision quaternion w + xi + yj + zk, mirroring cv::Quatd.
It is the standard representation for 3D rotations.

funcs.name: NewQuatd
funcs.signature: func NewQuatd(w, x, y, z float64) Quatd
funcs.doc: NewQuatd builds a Quatd from its four components.

funcs.name: QuatdFromAxisAngle
funcs.signature: func QuatdFromAxisAngle(axis Vec3d, angle float64) Quatd
funcs.doc:
QuatdFromAxisAngle builds a unit quaternion rotating by angle radians about
the given axis (which need not be normalized).

funcs.name: QuatdFromRotationMatrix
funcs.doc:
QuatdFromRotationMatrix builds a unit quaternion from a 3x3 rotation matrix
using the numerically stable branch of Shepperd's method.

funcs.name: QuatdIdentity
funcs.doc: QuatdIdentity returns the identity rotation quaternion (1, 0, 0, 0).

funcs.name: Slerpd
funcs.signature: func Slerpd(a, b Quatd, t float64) Quatd
funcs.doc:
Slerpd performs spherical linear interpolation between unit quaternions a and
b by parameter t in [0, 1].

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the component-wise sum q+o.

methods.name: Conjugate
methods.doc: Conjugate returns the conjugate (w, -x, -y, -z).

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the 4D dot product q·o.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether q and o have identical components.

methods.name: Inverse
methods.doc: Inverse returns the multiplicative inverse conj(q)/|q|².

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the Hamilton product q*o.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the quaternion norm |q|.

methods.name: NormSq
methods.doc: NormSq returns the squared norm |q|².

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc: Normalize returns the unit quaternion; a zero quaternion is returned as-is.

methods.name: RotateVector
methods.doc: RotateVector applies the rotation encoded by q to vector v.

methods.name: ScalarMul
methods.doc: ScalarMul returns the quaternion scaled by s.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the quaternion.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the component-wise difference q-o.

methods.name: ToRotationMatrix
methods.doc:
ToRotationMatrix returns the 3x3 rotation matrix of the unit quaternion. The
quaternion is normalized first.

types.name: Quatf
types.doc: Quatf is the float32 analogue of Quatd, mirroring cv::Quatf.

funcs.name: NewQuatf
funcs.signature: func NewQuatf(w, x, y, z float32) Quatf
funcs.doc: NewQuatf builds a Quatf from its four components.

funcs.name: QuatfIdentity
funcs.doc: QuatfIdentity returns the identity rotation quaternion (1, 0, 0, 0).

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the component-wise sum q+o.

methods.name: Conjugate
methods.doc: Conjugate returns the conjugate (w, -x, -y, -z).

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the Hamilton product q*o.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the quaternion norm |q|.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc: Normalize returns the unit quaternion; a zero quaternion is returned as-is.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the quaternion.

methods.name: ToQuatd
methods.doc: ToQuatd converts to a double-precision quaternion.

types.name: RNG
types.doc:
RNG is a fast pseudo-random number generator using the multiply-with-carry
scheme of OpenCV's cv::RNG. Given the same seed it produces the same
deterministic stream on every platform.

funcs.name: NewRNG
funcs.doc:
NewRNG creates an RNG seeded with the given value. A zero seed is replaced by
the OpenCV default so the generator never degenerates.

methods.name: FillGaussian
methods.doc:
FillGaussian fills dst with independent normal samples of the given standard
deviation and zero mean.

methods.name: FillUniformd
methods.doc: FillUniformd fills dst with independent uniform samples in [a, b).

methods.name: Gaussian
methods.doc:
Gaussian returns a normally distributed value with mean 0 and the given
standard deviation, using the Box–Muller transform.

methods.name: Next
methods.doc: Next returns the next 32-bit pseudo-random value and advances the state.

methods.name: NextBool
methods.doc: NextBool returns a pseudo-random boolean.

methods.name: Uniformd
methods.doc:
Uniformd returns a uniformly distributed float64 in the half-open range
[a, b).

methods.name: Uniformf
methods.doc:
Uniformf returns a uniformly distributed float32 in the half-open range
[a, b).

methods.name: Uniformi
methods.doc:
Uniformi returns a uniformly distributed integer in the half-open range
[a, b). It panics if b is not greater than a.

types.name: RNGMT19937
types.doc:
RNGMT19937 is a Mersenne Twister generator matching OpenCV's
cv::RNG_MT19937, offering a longer period than [RNG].

funcs.name: NewRNGMT19937
funcs.doc: NewRNGMT19937 creates a Mersenne Twister seeded with the given value.

methods.name: Next
methods.doc: Next returns the next 32-bit pseudo-random value.

methods.name: Uniformd
methods.doc:
Uniformd returns a uniformly distributed float64 in the half-open range
[a, b).

methods.name: Uniformi
methods.doc:
Uniformi returns a uniformly distributed integer in the half-open range
[a, b). It panics if b is not greater than a.

types.name: Range
types.doc: Range is a half-open integer interval [Start, End), mirroring cv::Range.

funcs.name: AllRange
funcs.doc:
AllRange returns the special range covering an entire dimension, encoded as
[MinInt, MaxInt) the way OpenCV's Range::all does.

funcs.name: NewRange
funcs.doc: NewRange builds a Range from start and end.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether i lies in [Start, End).

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc: Empty reports whether the range contains no elements.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether two ranges are identical.

methods.name: Intersect
methods.doc: Intersect returns the overlap of r and o; an empty range when disjoint.

methods.name: Shift
methods.doc: Shift returns the range translated by delta.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns End-Start.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the range as [Start, End).

types.name: Rect2d
types.doc: Rect2d is an axis-aligned rectangle with float64 fields, mirroring cv::Rect2d.

funcs.name: BoundingRect2d
funcs.doc:
BoundingRect2d returns the smallest upright rectangle containing every point.
It panics on an empty slice.

funcs.name: Rc2d
funcs.signature: func Rc2d(x, y, w, h float64) Rect2d
funcs.doc: Rc2d builds a Rect2d from its corner and size.

methods.name: And
methods.doc: And returns the intersection of r and o; an empty rectangle when disjoint.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns Width*Height.

methods.name: BR
methods.doc: BR returns the bottom-right corner.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether point p lies inside the rectangle.

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc: Empty reports whether the rectangle has non-positive area.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether two rectangles are identical.

methods.name: Or
methods.doc: Or returns the smallest rectangle containing both r and o.

methods.name: Shift
methods.doc: Shift returns the rectangle translated by point p.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns the rectangle's size.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the rectangle as [WxH from (X, Y)].

methods.name: TL
methods.doc: TL returns the top-left corner.

types.name: Rect2f
types.doc: Rect2f is an axis-aligned rectangle with float32 fields, mirroring cv::Rect2f.

funcs.name: BoundingRect2f
funcs.doc:
BoundingRect2f returns the smallest upright rectangle containing every point.
It panics on an empty slice.

funcs.name: Rc2f
funcs.signature: func Rc2f(x, y, w, h float32) Rect2f
funcs.doc: Rc2f builds a Rect2f from its corner and size.

methods.name: And
methods.doc: And returns the intersection of r and o; an empty rectangle when disjoint.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns Width*Height.

methods.name: BR
methods.doc: BR returns the bottom-right corner.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether point p lies inside the rectangle.

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc: Empty reports whether the rectangle has non-positive area.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether two rectangles are identical.

methods.name: Or
methods.doc: Or returns the smallest rectangle containing both r and o.

methods.name: Shift
methods.doc: Shift returns the rectangle translated by point p.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns the rectangle's size.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the rectangle as [WxH from (X, Y)].

methods.name: TL
methods.doc: TL returns the top-left corner.

types.name: Rect2i
types.doc:
Rect2i is an axis-aligned rectangle with an integer top-left corner and
integer dimensions, mirroring cv::Rect. The region covers the columns
[X, X+Width) and rows [Y, Y+Height).

funcs.name: BoundingRect2i
funcs.doc:
BoundingRect2i returns the smallest upright rectangle containing every point.
Width and Height are inclusive of the extreme pixels, so a single point gives
a 1x1 rectangle. It panics on an empty slice.

funcs.name: Rc2i
funcs.signature: func Rc2i(x, y, w, h int) Rect2i
funcs.doc: Rc2i builds a Rect2i from its corner and size.

methods.name: And
methods.doc:
And returns the intersection of r and o; an empty rectangle when they do not
overlap. This mirrors OpenCV's Rect & operator.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns Width*Height.

methods.name: BR
methods.doc: BR returns the bottom-right corner (exclusive: X+Width, Y+Height).

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc: Contains reports whether point p lies inside the rectangle.

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc: Empty reports whether the rectangle has non-positive area.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether two rectangles are identical.

methods.name: Or
methods.doc:
Or returns the smallest rectangle containing both r and o. When one operand
is empty the other is returned, mirroring OpenCV's Rect | operator.

methods.name: Shift
methods.doc: Shift returns the rectangle translated by point p.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns the rectangle's size.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the rectangle as [WxH from (X, Y)].

methods.name: TL
methods.doc: TL returns the top-left corner.

types.name: RotatedRect
types.doc:
RotatedRect is a rectangle rotated about its centre, mirroring
cv::RotatedRect. Center is in fractional pixels, Size holds the side lengths
and Angle is the clockwise rotation in degrees.

funcs.name: NewRotatedRect
funcs.signature: func NewRotatedRect(center Point2f, size Size2f, angle float64) RotatedRect
funcs.doc: NewRotatedRect builds a RotatedRect from its centre, size and angle.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns the rectangle's area (Width*Height).

methods.name: BoundingRect
methods.doc:
BoundingRect returns the smallest upright integer rectangle enclosing the
rotated rectangle.

methods.name: Points
methods.doc:
Points returns the four corners of the rotated rectangle in order
(bottom-left, top-left, top-right, bottom-right in the rectangle's frame).

types.name: Scalar
types.signature: type Scalar [4]float64
types.doc:
Scalar is a 4-element double vector, mirroring cv::Scalar. It is commonly
used to carry per-channel values such as colours or means.

funcs.name: NewScalar
funcs.doc:
NewScalar builds a Scalar from up to four components; missing entries are
zero and extra entries are ignored.

funcs.name: ScalarAll
funcs.doc: ScalarAll returns a Scalar with every component set to v.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the element-wise sum s+o.

methods.name: Conj
methods.doc: Conj returns the quaternion-style conjugate (v0, -v1, -v2, -v3).

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether two scalars are identical.

methods.name: IsReal
methods.doc: IsReal reports whether the last three components are all zero.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the scalar scaled by k.

methods.name: MulElem
methods.doc: MulElem returns the element-wise product s*o.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the scalar as [a, b, c, d].

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the element-wise difference s-o.

types.name: Size2d
types.doc: Size2d is a width/height pair with float64 dimensions, mirroring cv::Size2d.

funcs.name: Sz2d
funcs.doc: Sz2d builds a Size2d from width and height.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the element-wise sum of two sizes.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns Width*Height.

methods.name: Aspect
methods.doc: Aspect returns Width/Height as a float64 (0 when Height is 0).

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc: Empty reports whether either dimension is non-positive.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether two sizes are identical.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the size with both dimensions multiplied by k.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the size as WxH.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the element-wise difference of two sizes.

types.name: Size2f
types.doc: Size2f is a width/height pair with float32 dimensions, mirroring cv::Size2f.

funcs.name: Sz2f
funcs.doc: Sz2f builds a Size2f from width and height.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the element-wise sum of two sizes.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns Width*Height.

methods.name: Aspect
methods.doc: Aspect returns Width/Height as a float64 (0 when Height is 0).

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc: Empty reports whether either dimension is non-positive.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether two sizes are identical.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the size with both dimensions multiplied by k.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the size as WxH.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the element-wise difference of two sizes.

types.name: Size2i
types.doc: Size2i is a width/height pair with integer dimensions, mirroring cv::Size.

funcs.name: Sz2i
funcs.doc: Sz2i builds a Size2i from width and height.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the element-wise sum of two sizes.

methods.name: Area
methods.doc: Area returns Width*Height.

methods.name: Aspect
methods.doc: Aspect returns Width/Height as a float64 (0 when Height is 0).

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc: Empty reports whether either dimension is non-positive.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether two sizes are identical.

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns the size with both dimensions multiplied by k (truncated).

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the size as WxH.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the element-wise difference of two sizes.

types.name: TermCriteria
types.doc:
TermCriteria describes when an iterative algorithm should stop, mirroring
cv::TermCriteria.

funcs.name: NewTermCriteria
funcs.signature: func NewTermCriteria(t TermCriteriaType, maxCount int, eps float64) TermCriteria
funcs.doc:
NewTermCriteria builds a TermCriteria from its type flags, iteration cap and
epsilon.

methods.name: IsValid
methods.doc:
IsValid reports whether at least one stopping condition is enabled with a
usable bound.

types.name: TermCriteriaType
types.signature: type TermCriteriaType int
types.doc:
TermCriteriaType is a bit flag selecting the stopping conditions of an
iterative algorithm, mirroring cv::TermCriteria::Type.

types.name: Vec2b
types.signature: type Vec2b [2]uint8
types.doc:
Vec2b is a fixed-length vector of 2 uint8 values, mirroring
OpenCV's cv::Vec2b. Elements are stored in index order.

funcs.name: NewVec2b
funcs.signature: func NewVec2b(x uint8, y uint8) Vec2b
funcs.doc: NewVec2b builds a Vec2b from its 2 components.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the element-wise sum v+o (no saturation; uint8 arithmetic wraps).

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns element i of the vector. It panics if i is out of range.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot product of v and o accumulated in float64.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether v and o have identical components.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the vector scaled by s.

methods.name: MulElem
methods.doc: MulElem returns the element-wise (Hadamard) product v*o.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean (L2) length of the vector.

methods.name: NormL1
methods.doc: NormL1 returns the L1 (Manhattan) norm as a float64.

methods.name: NormSq
methods.doc: NormSq returns the squared L2 norm (v dot v) in float64.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the vector as [a, b, ...].

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the element-wise difference v-o.

methods.name: ToSlice
methods.doc: ToSlice returns the components as a freshly allocated slice.

methods.name: ToVec2d
methods.doc: ToVec2d converts the components to float64.

methods.name: ToVec2f
methods.doc: ToVec2f converts the components to float32.

types.name: Vec2d
types.signature: type Vec2d [2]float64
types.doc:
Vec2d is a fixed-length vector of 2 float64 values, mirroring
OpenCV's cv::Vec2d. Elements are stored in index order.

funcs.name: NewVec2d
funcs.signature: func NewVec2d(x float64, y float64) Vec2d
funcs.doc: NewVec2d builds a Vec2d from its 2 components.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the element-wise sum v+o (no saturation; float64 arithmetic wraps).

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns element i of the vector. It panics if i is out of range.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot product of v and o accumulated in float64.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether v and o have identical components.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the vector scaled by s.

methods.name: MulElem
methods.doc: MulElem returns the element-wise (Hadamard) product v*o.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean (L2) length of the vector.

methods.name: NormL1
methods.doc: NormL1 returns the L1 (Manhattan) norm as a float64.

methods.name: NormSq
methods.doc: NormSq returns the squared L2 norm (v dot v) in float64.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:

vector is returned unchanged.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the vector as [a, b, ...].

methods.name: Sub

methods.doc: ToSlice returns the components as a freshly allocated slice.

methods.name: ToVec2f
methods.doc: ToVec2f converts the components to float32.

OpenCV's cv::Vec2f. Elements are stored in index order.

funcs.signature: func NewVec2f(x float32, y float32) Vec2f
funcs.doc: NewVec2f builds a Vec2f from its 2 components.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the element-wise sum v+o (no saturation; float32 arithmetic wraps).

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns element i of the vector. It panics if i is out of range.

methods.doc: Dot returns the dot product of v and o accumulated in float64.

methods.name: MulElem

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean (L2) length of the vector.

methods.name: NormL1

Vec6d is a fixed-length vector of 6 float64 values, mirroring
OpenCV's cv::Vec6d. Elements are stored in index order.

funcs.name: NewVec6d
funcs.signature: func NewVec6d(x float64, y float64, z float64, w float64, a float64, b float64) Vec6d
funcs.doc: NewVec6d builds a Vec6d from its 6 components.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the element-wise sum v+o (no saturation; float64 arithmetic wraps).

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns element i of the vector. It panics if i is out of range.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot product of v and o accumulated in float64.

methods.name: Equals
methods.doc: Equals reports whether v and o have identical components.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc: Mul returns the vector scaled by s.

methods.name: MulElem
methods.doc: MulElem returns the element-wise (Hadamard) product v*o.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc: Norm returns the Euclidean (L2) length of the vector.

methods.name: NormL1
methods.doc: NormL1 returns the L1 (Manhattan) norm as a float64.

methods.name: NormSq
methods.doc: NormSq returns the squared L2 norm (v dot v) in float64.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns the unit vector in the direction of v. A zero
vector is returned unchanged.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the vector as [a, b, ...].

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the element-wise difference v-o.

methods.name: ToSlice
methods.doc: ToSlice returns the components as a freshly allocated slice.

methods.name: ToVec6f
methods.doc: ToVec6f converts the components to float32.

funcs.name: Angle2f
funcs.doc:
Angle2f returns the direction of the vector from a to b in radians, measured
with math.Atan2.

funcs.name: Dist2d
funcs.doc: Dist2d returns the Euclidean distance between two float64 points.

funcs.name: Dist2f
funcs.doc: Dist2f returns the Euclidean distance between two float32 points.

funcs.name: Dist2i
funcs.doc: Dist2i returns the Euclidean distance between two integer points.

funcs.name: Dist3d
funcs.doc: Dist3d returns the Euclidean distance between two 3D float64 points.

funcs.name: IsConvex2f
funcs.doc:
IsConvex2f reports whether the closed polygon is convex, i.e. all its turns
have the same orientation. Polygons with fewer than three vertices are
treated as convex.

funcs.name: ManhattanDist2i
funcs.doc:
ManhattanDist2i returns the L1 (city-block) distance between two integer
points.

funcs.name: Perimeter2f
funcs.doc:
Perimeter2f returns the total edge length of a point sequence. When closed is
true the segment from the last point back to the first is included.

funcs.name: PointInTriangle2f
funcs.signature: func PointInTriangle2f(p, a, b, c Point2f) bool
funcs.doc:
PointInTriangle2f reports whether point p lies inside (or on the boundary of)
triangle a,b,c.

funcs.name: PointToSegmentDist2f
funcs.signature: func PointToSegmentDist2f(p, a, b Point2f) float64
funcs.doc:
PointToSegmentDist2f returns the shortest distance from point p to the
segment a-b.

funcs.name: PolygonArea2f
funcs.doc: PolygonArea2f returns the absolute area of the polygon.

funcs.name: SignedPolygonArea2f
funcs.doc:
SignedPolygonArea2f returns the signed area of the polygon by the shoelace
formula; the sign is positive when the vertices wind counter-clockwise in a
y-up frame (negative in image coordinates where y grows downward).

packages.name: cudaarithm
packages.synopsis: Package cudaarithm is a CPU-backed, API-compatible mirror of OpenCV's cudaarithm module.
packages.doc:
Package cudaarithm is a CPU-backed, API-compatible mirror of OpenCV's
cudaarithm module. It provides the shape of OpenCV's CUDA per-element and
matrix-reduction API — a [GpuMat] device-matrix type, a no-op async [Stream],
and a large family of element-wise, mathematical, channel and reduction
operations — implemented entirely in pure Go on top of the root module
github.com/malcolmston/opencv (imported as cv) and the Go standard library.

# Honest scope: there is no GPU

This package performs NO GPU computation. There is no cgo, no CUDA runtime,
and no device memory. Every operation runs on the CPU against the ordinary
host-resident [cv.Mat] that a [GpuMat] wraps. The CUDA vocabulary is
reproduced so that code written against OpenCV's cudaarithm reads and ports
naturally, but the following are deliberate, honest fictions:

  - [GpuMat] is a thin wrapper around a *cv.Mat. "Uploading" and
    "downloading" ([GpuMat.Upload], [GpuMat.Download], [NewGpuMat]) copy the
    matrix rather than moving it across a PCIe bus, because host and device
    memory are the same memory here. The copy semantics are preserved (the
    returned/stored Mat does not alias the caller's), which keeps behaviour
    predictable, but there is no acceleration.
  - [Stream] is a no-op. Every operation accepts an optional trailing
    stream argument for source compatibility, but work is always performed
    synchronously before the call returns, so [Stream.WaitForCompletion] has
    nothing to wait for.

# The uint8 constraint

The root package's [cv.Mat] stores 8-bit unsigned samples (depth CV_8U).
This package therefore operates on 8-bit matrices and, like the root
package's own arithmetic, rounds by adding 0.5 and truncating toward zero
before saturating into [0,255]. Operations whose mathematical range exceeds a
byte — [Pow], [Exp], [Gemm], [Magnitude] and friends — saturate their
results; each documents this. The Fourier routines [DFT], [IDFT] and
[MulSpectrums] cannot represent complex spectra in a uint8 matrix at all, so
they exchange the dedicated float-valued [ComplexMat] type instead of a
[GpuMat]; this is the one place the API shape necessarily diverges, and it is
documented on those functions.

# Operation families

Element-wise: [Add], [Subtract], [Multiply], [Divide], [AbsDiff],
[AddWeighted], [BitwiseAnd], [BitwiseOr], [BitwiseXor], [BitwiseNot], [Min],
[Max], [Compare], [Threshold] and [Abs] mirror the root package's saturating
arithmetic on [GpuMat] operands.

Mathematical: [Pow], [Exp], [Log], [Sqrt], [Magnitude], [Phase],
[CartToPolar] and [PolarToCart] compute in float64 and saturate to 8 bits.

Channel and layout: [Split], [Merge], [Transpose], [Flip], [LUT] and
[Normalize] rearrange or remap samples.

Reductions: [Sum], [AbsSum], [SqrSum], [MinMax], [MinMaxLoc], [CountNonZero],
[Mean], [MeanStdDev] and [Norm] collapse a matrix to scalars.

Linear algebra and spectra: [Gemm] is a saturating general matrix multiply;
[DFT]/[IDFT] are a genuine (naive, exact) discrete Fourier transform and its
inverse, and [MulSpectrums] multiplies two spectra element-wise.

All functions validate their arguments and panic with a descriptive message
on programmer error (mismatched shapes, empty inputs), matching the root
package's convention. Inputs are never mutated; every result is freshly
allocated.

types.name: CmpOp
types.signature: type CmpOp int
types.doc: CmpOp selects the relation applied by [Compare].

types.name: ComplexMat
types.doc:
ComplexMat is a dense, row-major complex matrix used by [DFT], [IDFT] and
[MulSpectrums]. It exists because a [GpuMat]'s underlying [cv.Mat] stores only
8-bit samples and cannot represent the range of Fourier coefficients; this is
the one place the CUDA-mirror API necessarily diverges from operating on a
GpuMat. Re and Im each have length Rows*Cols.

funcs.name: DFT
funcs.doc:
DFT computes the forward two-dimensional discrete Fourier transform of a
single-channel GpuMat and returns the complex spectrum, mirroring
cv::cuda::dft. The transform is a genuine, exact DFT (a direct evaluation of
the definition, not a fast-transform approximation), so it is O((rows*cols)^2)
and intended for modest sizes. Real input is taken from the samples; the
imaginary part starts at zero. It panics unless src is single-channel.

funcs.name: IDFTComplex
funcs.doc:
IDFTComplex computes the inverse two-dimensional DFT and returns the full
complex result (normalised by 1/(rows*cols)) without rounding to bytes. It is
useful when the inverse transform is an intermediate result rather than a
displayable image.

funcs.name: MulSpectrums
funcs.doc:
MulSpectrums multiplies two spectra element-wise, mirroring
cv::cuda::mulSpectrums. When conjB is true the complex conjugate of b is used,
which corresponds to correlation rather than convolution. The operands must
share a shape. Combined with [DFT] and [IDFT] this realises the convolution
theorem: IDFT(MulSpectrums(DFT(a), DFT(b))) is the circular convolution of a
and b.

funcs.name: NewComplexMat
funcs.doc: NewComplexMat allocates a zero-filled ComplexMat.

methods.name: At
methods.signature: func (c *ComplexMat) At(y, x int) (re, im float64)
methods.doc: At returns the real and imaginary parts at row y, column x.

types.name: GpuMat
types.doc:
GpuMat is a CPU-backed stand-in for OpenCV's cv::cuda::GpuMat. It wraps a
host-resident [cv.Mat]; there is no device memory and no GPU. The wrapped Mat
is unexported so that callers move data through [GpuMat.Upload] and
[GpuMat.Download], preserving OpenCV's host/device copy discipline, but it is
reachable read-only through [GpuMat.Mat] for interop with the root package.

funcs.name: Abs
funcs.doc:
Abs returns |src| per sample. Because [cv.Mat] samples are unsigned, every
value is already non-negative, so this returns a copy of src. It exists for
API parity with cv::cuda::abs.

funcs.name: AbsDiff
funcs.doc:
AbsDiff returns the per-sample absolute difference |a-b|, delegating to
[cv.AbsDiff]. Operands must share a shape.

funcs.name: Add
funcs.doc:
Add returns the per-sample saturating sum a+b as a new GpuMat. It delegates to
the root package's [cv.Add]. The optional stream is accepted for API
compatibility and ignored (work is synchronous). Operands must share a shape.

funcs.name: AddWeighted
funcs.signature: func AddWeighted(a *GpuMat, alpha float64, b *GpuMat, beta, gamma float64, _ ...*Stream) *GpuMat
funcs.doc:
AddWeighted returns the per-sample weighted sum alpha*a + beta*b + gamma,
rounded and saturated, delegating to [cv.AddWeighted]. Operands must share a
shape.

funcs.name: BitwiseAnd
funcs.doc:
BitwiseAnd returns the per-sample bitwise AND of a and b, delegating to
[cv.BitwiseAnd]. Operands must share a shape.

funcs.name: BitwiseNot
funcs.doc:
BitwiseNot returns the per-sample bitwise complement (255-value) of src,
delegating to [cv.BitwiseNot].

funcs.name: BitwiseOr
funcs.doc:
BitwiseOr returns the per-sample bitwise OR of a and b, delegating to
[cv.BitwiseOr]. Operands must share a shape.

funcs.name: BitwiseXor
funcs.doc:
BitwiseXor returns the per-sample bitwise XOR of a and b, delegating to
[cv.BitwiseXor]. Operands must share a shape.

funcs.name: CartToPolar
funcs.signature: func CartToPolar(x, y *GpuMat, angleInDegrees bool, _ ...*Stream) (magnitude, angle *GpuMat)
funcs.doc:
CartToPolar converts the Cartesian vectors (x, y) to polar form, returning the
magnitude and angle GpuMats, mirroring cv::cuda::cartToPolar. See [Magnitude]
and [Phase] for the saturation behaviour of each output. Inputs must share a
shape.

funcs.name: Compare
funcs.doc:
Compare compares a and b per sample and returns a mask that is 255 where the
relation op holds and 0 elsewhere, matching cv::cuda::compare. Operands must
share a shape.

funcs.name: Divide
funcs.doc:
Divide returns the per-sample quotient scale*a/b (0 where b is 0), rounded and
saturated, delegating to [cv.Divide]. Operands must share a shape.

funcs.name: Exp
funcs.doc:
Exp returns e raised to each sample, rounded and saturated, mirroring
cv::cuda::exp. Because exp grows quickly, samples above ~5 saturate to 255.

funcs.name: Flip
funcs.doc:
Flip mirrors src along the axis chosen by code, mirroring cv::cuda::flip. It
delegates to [cv.Flip]; code is a [cv.FlipCode].

funcs.name: Gemm
funcs.signature: func Gemm(a, b *GpuMat, alpha float64, c *GpuMat, beta float64, _ ...*Stream) *GpuMat
funcs.doc:
Gemm computes the saturating general matrix product alpha*A*B + beta*C and
returns it as a single-channel GpuMat, mirroring cv::cuda::gemm (without the
transpose flags). A must be m×k, B must be k×n and, when non-nil, C must be
m×n; all operands must be single-channel. Pass a nil c to omit the beta*C
term. Products can easily exceed a byte, so results are rounded and saturated
into [0,255]; keep operands small for exact results.

funcs.name: IDFT
funcs.doc:
IDFT computes the inverse two-dimensional DFT of a spectrum and returns the
real part as a rounded, saturated single-channel GpuMat, mirroring
cv::cuda::dft with the inverse flag. The inverse is normalised by 1/(rows*cols)
so that IDFT(DFT(x)) reproduces x (up to rounding). Negative real parts clamp
to 0 and parts above 255 clamp to 255.

funcs.name: LUT
funcs.doc:
LUT remaps every sample through a 256-entry lookup table, mirroring
cv::cuda::LUT (the OpenCV CUDA LookUpTable). table must have exactly 256
entries; the same table applies to every channel. It panics otherwise.

funcs.name: Log
funcs.doc:
Log returns the natural logarithm of each sample, rounded and saturated,
mirroring cv::cuda::log. A zero sample, whose true log is -inf, maps to 0.

funcs.name: Magnitude
funcs.doc:
Magnitude returns the per-sample vector magnitude sqrt(x^2 + y^2), rounded and
saturated, mirroring cv::cuda::magnitude. Inputs must share a shape. Because
the result is a byte, magnitudes above 255 saturate.

funcs.name: Max
funcs.doc:
Max returns the per-sample maximum of a and b, delegating to [cv.Max].
Operands must share a shape.

funcs.name: Merge
funcs.doc:
Merge interleaves single-channel GpuMats into one multi-channel GpuMat,
mirroring cv::cuda::merge. Every plane must be single-channel and share a
shape. It delegates to [cv.Merge].

funcs.name: Min
funcs.doc:
Min returns the per-sample minimum of a and b, delegating to [cv.Min].
Operands must share a shape.

funcs.name: Multiply
funcs.doc:
Multiply returns the per-sample product a*b*scale, rounded and saturated,
delegating to [cv.Multiply]. Operands must share a shape.

funcs.name: NewGpuMat
funcs.doc:
NewGpuMat returns a GpuMat holding a copy of src, mimicking an upload of a
host matrix to the device. Because there is no device memory the "upload" is
a deep copy: mutating src afterwards does not affect the GpuMat. A nil src
yields an empty GpuMat.

funcs.name: Normalize
funcs.signature: func Normalize(src *GpuMat, alpha, beta float64, normType NormType, _ ...*Stream) *GpuMat
funcs.doc:
Normalize rescales src according to normType and returns a new GpuMat,
mirroring cv::cuda::normalize. For [NormMinMax] the sample range is mapped
linearly onto [alpha, beta] (delegating to [cv.Normalize]). For [NormL1],
[NormL2] and [NormInf] every sample is scaled by alpha divided by the
corresponding norm of src, so the result has that norm equal to alpha; beta is
unused. Results are rounded and saturated into [0,255].

funcs.name: Phase
funcs.doc:
Phase returns the per-sample orientation atan2(y, x) of the vectors, mirroring
cv::cuda::phase. When angleInDegrees is false the angle is in radians (0..2π,
so it saturates to a byte above 6); when true it is in degrees (0..360, so it
saturates above 255). The result is rounded and saturated. Inputs must share a
shape.

funcs.name: PolarToCart
funcs.signature: func PolarToCart(magnitude, angle *GpuMat, angleInDegrees bool, _ ...*Stream) (x, y *GpuMat)
funcs.doc:
PolarToCart converts the polar vectors (magnitude, angle) back to Cartesian
form, returning the x and y GpuMats, mirroring cv::cuda::polarToCart. angle is
interpreted in degrees when angleInDegrees is true, otherwise radians. Outputs
are rounded and saturated into [0,255], so components of vectors pointing into
negative quadrants clamp to 0. Inputs must share a shape.

funcs.name: Pow
funcs.doc:
Pow raises each sample of src to power and returns the rounded, saturated
result, mirroring cv::cuda::pow. The computation is on the raw 0..255 sample
value, so with power > 1 all but the smallest samples saturate to 255; with a
fractional power such as 0.5 the result stays in range. A negative base with a
non-integer power yields 0.

funcs.name: Split
funcs.doc:
Split separates a multi-channel GpuMat into one single-channel GpuMat per
channel, in channel order, mirroring cv::cuda::split. It delegates to
[cv.Mat.Split].

funcs.name: Sqrt
funcs.doc:
Sqrt returns the square root of each sample, rounded and saturated, mirroring
cv::cuda::sqrt. Results always fit in a byte (sqrt(255) < 16).

funcs.name: Subtract
funcs.doc:
Subtract returns the per-sample saturating difference a-b (floored at 0),
delegating to [cv.Subtract]. Operands must share a shape.

funcs.name: Threshold
funcs.signature: func Threshold(src *GpuMat, thresh, maxval float64, typ cv.ThresholdType, _ ...*Stream) (*GpuMat, float64)
funcs.doc:
Threshold applies a fixed-level threshold to a single-channel GpuMat and
returns the thresholded result together with the threshold used, delegating to
[cv.Threshold]. typ is a [cv.ThresholdType] (optionally OR-ed with
[cv.ThreshOtsu]).

funcs.name: Transpose
funcs.doc:
Transpose swaps rows and columns, returning a new GpuMat of shape Cols×Rows,
mirroring cv::cuda::transpose. It delegates to [cv.Transpose].

methods.name: Channels
methods.doc: Channels returns the number of channels, or 0 when empty.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns a deep copy of the GpuMat with its own backing storage.

methods.name: Download
methods.doc:
Download returns a copy of the GpuMat's contents as a host [cv.Mat], the
analogue of cv::cuda::GpuMat::download. The returned Mat is an independent
deep copy; it is nil for an empty GpuMat. No actual device-to-host transfer
occurs because the data already lives in host memory.

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc: Empty reports whether the GpuMat holds no samples.

methods.name: Mat
methods.doc:
Mat returns the wrapped [cv.Mat] without copying, for read-only interop with
the root package. Callers must not mutate it; use [GpuMat.Download] for an
owned copy. It is nil for an empty GpuMat.

methods.name: Release
methods.doc:
Release frees the GpuMat's contents, leaving it empty. It mirrors
cv::cuda::GpuMat::release; here it simply drops the reference so the garbage
collector can reclaim the samples.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns the matrix dimensions as (rows, cols), or (0, 0) when empty.

methods.name: Type
methods.doc:
Type returns the [MatType] of the matrix. Every GpuMat has CV_8U depth, so
the type reflects only the channel count. An empty GpuMat reports CV_8UC1.

methods.name: Upload
methods.doc:
Upload copies src into the GpuMat, replacing any previous contents. It is the
analogue of cv::cuda::GpuMat::upload. As there is no device memory the copy is
a deep clone; it is a no-op transfer in the sense that no bus is crossed, but
it does duplicate the samples so the GpuMat never aliases src.

types.name: MatType
types.signature: type MatType int
types.doc:
MatType encodes an OpenCV matrix type. Because the root package's [cv.Mat]
only stores 8-bit unsigned samples, every [GpuMat] has depth CV_8U and its
type is fully determined by the channel count. The constants below name the
common cases; [MakeType] builds one for an arbitrary channel count.

consts.doc:
Depth CV_8U is 0 in OpenCV; a type with n channels is n-1 shifted left by the
depth-bit width (3). These constants match OpenCV's CV_8UC1..CV_8UC4 values.

funcs.name: MakeType
funcs.doc:
MakeType returns the CV_8U MatType for the given channel count, mirroring
OpenCV's CV_8UC(n). It panics if channels is not positive.

methods.name: Channels
methods.doc: Channels returns the number of channels encoded by the type.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the type as OpenCV's CV_8UC<n> spelling.

types.name: NormType
types.signature: type NormType int
types.doc: NormType selects the norm used by [Norm] and [Normalize].

types.name: Stream
types.signature: type Stream struct{}
types.doc:
Stream is a CPU-backed stand-in for cv::cuda::Stream. Every operation in this
package runs synchronously, so a Stream carries no state and enqueues no
work. It exists solely so that code written against the CUDA API — which
threads an optional stream through every call — ports without change.

funcs.name: NewStream
funcs.doc: NewStream returns a ready-to-use no-op Stream.

methods.name: WaitForCompletion
methods.doc:
WaitForCompletion returns immediately. Because operations complete
synchronously before they return, there is never any outstanding work to wait
for. It is provided for API compatibility with cv::cuda::Stream.

funcs.name: AbsSum
funcs.doc:
AbsSum returns the per-channel sum of absolute sample values, mirroring
cv::cuda::absSum. Because [cv.Mat] samples are unsigned this equals [Sum], but
the function is provided for API parity.

funcs.name: CountNonZero
funcs.doc:
CountNonZero returns the number of samples that are not zero, mirroring
cv::cuda::countNonZero. All channels are considered.

funcs.name: Mean
funcs.doc:
Mean returns the per-channel arithmetic mean of the samples, mirroring
cv::cuda::mean.

funcs.name: MeanStdDev
funcs.doc:
MeanStdDev returns the per-channel mean and population standard deviation of
the samples, mirroring cv::cuda::meanStdDev.

funcs.name: MinMax
funcs.doc:
MinMax returns the minimum and maximum sample value of a single-channel
GpuMat, mirroring cv::cuda::minMax. It panics unless src is single-channel.

funcs.name: MinMaxLoc
funcs.signature: func MinMaxLoc(src *GpuMat, _ ...*Stream) (minVal, maxVal float64, minX, minY, maxX, maxY int)
funcs.doc:
MinMaxLoc returns the minimum and maximum sample values of a single-channel
GpuMat together with the (x, y) location of the first occurrence of each,
mirroring cv::cuda::minMaxLoc. It panics unless src is single-channel.

funcs.name: Norm
funcs.doc:
Norm returns the norm of src selected by normType, mirroring cv::cuda::norm.
[NormMinMax] is not a valid measurement norm and panics.

funcs.name: SqrSum
funcs.doc:
SqrSum returns the per-channel sum of squared sample values, mirroring
cv::cuda::sqrSum.

funcs.name: Sum
funcs.doc:
Sum returns the per-channel sum of all samples, mirroring cv::cuda::sum. The
result has one entry per channel.

examples.code:
a := cv.NewMat(1, 4, 1)
copy(a.Data, []uint8{10, 90, 130, 250})
b := cv.NewMat(1, 4, 1)
copy(b.Data, []uint8{5, 30, 40, 10})

sum := cudaarithm.Add(cudaarithm.NewGpuMat(a), cudaarithm.NewGpuMat(b))
mask, _ := cudaarithm.Threshold(sum, 120, 255, cv.ThreshBinary)

fmt.Println(ints(sum.Download().Data))
fmt.Println(ints(mask.Download().Data))
examples.doc:
Example shows the end-to-end cudaarithm flow: upload two host matrices into
device matrices, add them with saturation, threshold the sum, then download
both results back to host memory.

examples.name: MinMaxLoc
examples.doc: ExampleMinMaxLoc reports the extreme sample values and their locations.

examples.name: Add
examples.doc:
ExampleAdd shows the upload / operate / download flow that mirrors OpenCV's
cuda API. No GPU is involved; the "device" matrices live in host memory.

examples.name: Gemm
examples.doc: ExampleGemm multiplies two small matrices with saturation.

examples.name: Stream
examples.doc:
ExampleStream shows that a Stream can be threaded through operations for
source compatibility; work still completes synchronously.

packages.name: cudabgsegm
packages.synopsis: Package cudabgsegm is a CPU-backed, API-compatible mirror of OpenCV's cudabgsegm module — the GPU background-segmentation subtractors that in upstream OpenCV live under the cv::cuda namespace (createBackgroundSubtractorMOG, createBackgroundSubtractorMOG2, createBackgroundSubtractorGMG and createBackgroundSubtractorFGD).
packages.doc:
Package cudabgsegm is a CPU-backed, API-compatible mirror of OpenCV's
cudabgsegm module — the GPU background-segmentation subtractors that in
upstream OpenCV live under the cv::cuda namespace (createBackgroundSubtractorMOG,
createBackgroundSubtractorMOG2, createBackgroundSubtractorGMG and
createBackgroundSubtractorFGD).

# Honest scope

There is no CUDA, no GPU and no cgo here. This package is pure Go built only
on the standard library, the root package [github.com/malcolmston/opencv]
(imported under the alias cv) and the sibling CPU package
[github.com/malcolmston/opencv/bgsegm], to which every model delegates. The
point is source and shape compatibility: code written against the OpenCV CUDA
bgsegm API — a [GpuMat] holding the frame, a [Stream] threaded through each
call, factory functions named createBackgroundSubtractor* and the matching
getter/setter methods — ports across with minimal edits, while the actual
pixels are classified on the CPU by the [github.com/malcolmston/opencv/bgsegm]
models.

Because the compute is identical to the CPU package, the GPU-specific
performance characteristics of the real module are not reproduced. The
[Stream] type is a no-op: it exists so signatures match, and it does nothing.
The per-call learningRate argument accepted by the MOG-family Apply methods is
likewise accepted for signature compatibility; the CPU models derive their own
adaptive rate from the configured history and frame count, so a caller-supplied
rate other than the OpenCV "auto" sentinel (-1) is documented where it is
ignored.

# Types

A [GpuMat] is a thin wrapper around a *[cv.Mat]. In real OpenCV a GpuMat lives
in device memory and must be uploaded to and downloaded from host memory
explicitly; here [GpuMat.Upload] and [GpuMat.Download] are ordinary copies, but
they are provided so the upload/download idiom compiles and runs unchanged.

# Models

  - [BackgroundSubtractorMOG]  — original KaewTraKulPong–Bowden Gaussian mixture,
    via [bgsegm.BackgroundSubtractorMOG].
  - [BackgroundSubtractorMOG2] — Zivkovic adaptive Gaussian mixture with shadow
    detection, via [bgsegm.BackgroundSubtractorMOG2].
  - [BackgroundSubtractorGMG]  — Godbehere–Matsukawa–Goldberg Bayesian model,
    via [bgsegm.BackgroundSubtractorGMG].

# Deferred

OpenCV's cudabgsegm also exposes createBackgroundSubtractorFGD (the Li et al.
foreground–background statistical model). Neither the [bgsegm] package nor the
root cv package supplies an FGD-equivalent model, so it is intentionally not
mirrored here. It is deferred until a CPU FGD implementation exists to delegate
to; adding it would otherwise mean writing the algorithm in this package, which
is out of scope for a thin compatibility mirror.

# Masks

Every Apply returns a fresh single-channel foreground mask wrapped in a
[GpuMat]; samples are [bgsegm.BackgroundValue] (0), [bgsegm.ForegroundValue]
(255) or, when shadow detection is enabled, [bgsegm.ShadowValue] (127).

types.name: BackgroundSubtractorGMG
types.doc:
BackgroundSubtractorGMG is a CPU-backed mirror of the subtractor returned by
OpenCV's cv::cuda::createBackgroundSubtractorGMG — the
Godbehere–Matsukawa–Goldberg Bayesian per-pixel model. It wraps a
[bgsegm.BackgroundSubtractorGMG] and exchanges frames and masks as [GpuMat]
values. GMG does not classify shadows.

Construct one with [CreateBackgroundSubtractorGMG]; the zero value is not
usable.

funcs.name: CreateBackgroundSubtractorGMG
funcs.doc:
CreateBackgroundSubtractorGMG creates a CUDA-style GMG subtractor, mirroring
cv::cuda::createBackgroundSubtractorGMG(initializationFrames,
decisionThreshold). Both arguments fall back to the OpenCV defaults (20 and
0.8) when non-positive.

methods.name: Apply
methods.doc:
Apply learns from or classifies frame and returns the foreground mask wrapped
in a fresh [GpuMat]. During the initialization period the mask is all
background. It mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorGMG::apply(image, fgmask, learningRate, stream).

learningRate is accepted for signature compatibility; the CPU model uses its
own configured aging rate and ignores any value other than the OpenCV auto
sentinel (-1). The stream is a no-op. Apply panics on a nil or empty frame, or
on a frame whose size differs from the first frame applied.

methods.name: GetBackgroundImage
methods.doc:
GetBackgroundImage returns the model's current background estimate wrapped in a
[GpuMat]. The returned GpuMat is empty before the first
[BackgroundSubtractorGMG.Apply]. The stream is a no-op.

methods.name: GetDecisionThreshold
methods.doc:
GetDecisionThreshold returns the foreground-probability threshold above which a
pixel is classified as foreground. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorGMG::getDecisionThreshold.

methods.name: GetNumFrames
methods.doc:
GetNumFrames returns the length of the initial learning period. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorGMG::getNumFrames.

methods.name: SetDecisionThreshold
methods.doc:
SetDecisionThreshold sets the foreground-probability threshold above which a
pixel is classified as foreground. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorGMG::setDecisionThreshold.

methods.name: SetNumFrames
methods.doc:
SetNumFrames sets the length of the initial learning period. It only takes
effect before the first Apply. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorGMG::setNumFrames.

types.name: BackgroundSubtractorMOG
types.doc:
BackgroundSubtractorMOG is a CPU-backed mirror of the subtractor returned by
OpenCV's cv::cuda::createBackgroundSubtractorMOG — the original
KaewTraKulPong–Bowden adaptive Gaussian-mixture model. It wraps a
[bgsegm.BackgroundSubtractorMOG] and exchanges frames and masks as [GpuMat]
values.

Construct one with [CreateBackgroundSubtractorMOG]; the zero value is not
usable.

funcs.name: CreateBackgroundSubtractorMOG
funcs.signature: func CreateBackgroundSubtractorMOG(history, nmixtures int, backgroundRatio, noiseSigma float64) *BackgroundSubtractorMOG
funcs.doc:
CreateBackgroundSubtractorMOG creates a CUDA-style MOG subtractor, mirroring
cv::cuda::createBackgroundSubtractorMOG(history, nmixtures, backgroundRatio,
noiseSigma). history and nmixtures fall back to the OpenCV defaults (200 and 5)
when non-positive. A non-positive backgroundRatio or noiseSigma leaves the CPU
model's own defaults (0.7 and 15) in place, matching OpenCV's use of 0 as the
"auto noise" sentinel.

methods.name: Apply
methods.doc:
Apply classifies frame against the model, updates the model and returns the
foreground mask wrapped in a fresh [GpuMat]. It mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG::apply(image, fgmask, learningRate, stream).

learningRate is accepted for signature compatibility. The CPU model derives
its own adaptive rate from the configured history and the number of frames seen
so far, so any value other than the OpenCV auto sentinel (-1) is ignored. The
stream is a no-op. Apply panics on a nil or empty frame, or on a frame whose
size differs from the first frame applied.

methods.name: GetBackgroundImage
methods.doc:
GetBackgroundImage returns the model's current background estimate wrapped in a
[GpuMat], mirroring cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG::getBackgroundImage. The
returned GpuMat is empty before the first [BackgroundSubtractorMOG.Apply]. The
stream is a no-op.

methods.name: GetBackgroundRatio
methods.doc:
GetBackgroundRatio returns the fraction of mixture weight that defines the
background. Mirrors cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG::getBackgroundRatio.

methods.name: GetHistory
methods.doc:
GetHistory returns the model's history length. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG::getHistory.

methods.name: GetNMixtures
methods.doc:
GetNMixtures returns the number of Gaussians kept per pixel. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG::getNMixtures.

methods.name: GetNoiseSigma
methods.doc:
GetNoiseSigma returns the standard deviation assigned to a freshly spawned
Gaussian. Mirrors cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG::getNoiseSigma.

methods.name: SetBackgroundRatio
methods.doc:
SetBackgroundRatio sets the fraction of mixture weight that defines the
background. Mirrors cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG::setBackgroundRatio.

methods.name: SetHistory
methods.doc:
SetHistory sets the model's history length. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG::setHistory.

methods.name: SetNMixtures
methods.doc:
SetNMixtures sets the number of Gaussians kept per pixel. It only takes effect
before the first Apply, since the per-pixel mixtures are allocated then.
Mirrors cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG::setNMixtures.

methods.name: SetNoiseSigma
methods.doc:
SetNoiseSigma sets the standard deviation assigned to a freshly spawned
Gaussian. Mirrors cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG::setNoiseSigma.

types.name: BackgroundSubtractorMOG2
types.doc:
BackgroundSubtractorMOG2 is a CPU-backed mirror of the subtractor returned by
OpenCV's cv::cuda::createBackgroundSubtractorMOG2 — the Zivkovic adaptive
Gaussian-mixture model with optional shadow detection. It wraps a
[bgsegm.BackgroundSubtractorMOG2] and exchanges frames and masks as [GpuMat]
values.

Construct one with [CreateBackgroundSubtractorMOG2]; the zero value is not
usable.

funcs.name: CreateBackgroundSubtractorMOG2
funcs.signature: func CreateBackgroundSubtractorMOG2(history int, varThreshold float64, detectShadows bool) *BackgroundSubtractorMOG2
funcs.doc:
CreateBackgroundSubtractorMOG2 creates a CUDA-style MOG2 subtractor, mirroring
cv::cuda::createBackgroundSubtractorMOG2(history, varThreshold, detectShadows).
history and varThreshold fall back to the OpenCV defaults (500 and 16) when
non-positive; detectShadows toggles shadow classification.

methods.name: Apply
methods.doc:
Apply classifies frame against the model, updates the model and returns the
foreground mask wrapped in a fresh [GpuMat]. It mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG2::apply(image, fgmask, learningRate, stream).

learningRate is accepted for signature compatibility. The CPU model derives its
own adaptive rate from the configured history and the number of frames seen so
far, so any value other than the OpenCV auto sentinel (-1) is ignored. The
stream is a no-op. Apply panics on a nil or empty frame, or on a frame whose
size differs from the first frame applied.

methods.name: GetBackgroundImage
methods.doc:
GetBackgroundImage returns the model's current background estimate wrapped in a
[GpuMat], mirroring cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG2::getBackgroundImage. The
returned GpuMat is empty before the first [BackgroundSubtractorMOG2.Apply]. The
stream is a no-op.

methods.name: GetDetectShadows
methods.doc:
GetDetectShadows reports whether shadow classification is enabled. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG2::getDetectShadows.

methods.name: GetHistory
methods.doc:
GetHistory returns the model's history length. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG2::getHistory.

methods.name: GetVarThreshold
methods.doc:
GetVarThreshold returns the squared-Mahalanobis matching threshold. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG2::getVarThreshold.

methods.name: SetDetectShadows
methods.doc:
SetDetectShadows enables or disables shadow classification. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG2::setDetectShadows.

methods.name: SetHistory
methods.doc:
SetHistory sets the model's history length. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG2::setHistory.

methods.name: SetVarThreshold
methods.doc:
SetVarThreshold sets the squared-Mahalanobis matching threshold. Mirrors
cv::cuda::BackgroundSubtractorMOG2::setVarThreshold.

types.name: GpuMat
types.doc:
GpuMat is a CPU-backed stand-in for OpenCV's cv::cuda::GpuMat. In real OpenCV
a GpuMat holds pixels in device (GPU) memory; here it simply wraps a host-side
*[cv.Mat]. It exists so that code written against the CUDA API — which passes
frames and masks as GpuMat values and moves them between host and device with
Upload/Download — compiles and runs unchanged against this pure-Go port.

The zero value holds a nil Mat and is reported [GpuMat.Empty]. Construct usable
instances with [NewGpuMat] or [GpuMatFromMat].

funcs.name: GpuMatFromMat
funcs.doc:
GpuMatFromMat wraps an existing host matrix as a GpuMat without copying it.
The returned GpuMat shares storage with m; use [GpuMat.Clone] for an
independent copy. A nil m yields an empty GpuMat.

funcs.name: NewGpuMat
funcs.doc:
NewGpuMat returns an empty GpuMat with no backing matrix. It mirrors the
default-constructed cv::cuda::GpuMat.

methods.name: Channels
methods.doc: Channels returns the channel count of the wrapped matrix, or 0 when empty.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc:
Clone returns a deep copy of the GpuMat with its own backing matrix, mirroring
cv::cuda::GpuMat::clone. Cloning an empty GpuMat yields another empty GpuMat.

methods.name: Download
methods.doc:
Download returns a host copy of the wrapped matrix, standing in for
cv::cuda::GpuMat::download. It returns nil for an empty GpuMat. The optional
stream is accepted for API compatibility and ignored.

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc:
Empty reports whether the GpuMat has no samples, mirroring
cv::cuda::GpuMat::empty.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc:
Size returns the dimensions of the wrapped matrix as (rows, cols), or (0, 0)
when the GpuMat is empty.

methods.name: Upload
methods.doc:
Upload copies host matrix m into this GpuMat, standing in for
cv::cuda::GpuMat::upload. Because there is no device memory, the wrapped Mat is
replaced with a deep copy of m (or nil when m is nil). The optional stream is
accepted for API compatibility and ignored.

types.name: Stream
types.signature: type Stream struct{}
types.doc:
Stream is a CPU-backed no-op stand-in for OpenCV's cv::cuda::Stream. In real
OpenCV a Stream queues asynchronous GPU work; here there is no device and no
asynchrony, so a Stream carries no state and every operation on it returns
immediately. It exists purely so that the CUDA API's habit of threading a
Stream through each call compiles and runs unchanged.

A nil *Stream is valid everywhere a Stream is accepted and is treated as the
default (null) stream, mirroring cv::cuda::Stream::Null().

funcs.name: NewStream
funcs.doc:
NewStream returns a new no-op Stream, mirroring the cv::cuda::Stream
constructor.

methods.name: QueryIfComplete
methods.doc:
QueryIfComplete always reports true, since work on this backend is synchronous
and already finished by the time any call returns. It mirrors
cv::cuda::Stream::queryIfComplete.

methods.name: WaitForCompletion
methods.doc:
WaitForCompletion returns immediately: there is no asynchronous work to wait
for. It mirrors cv::cuda::Stream::waitForCompletion.

examples.doc:
Example trains the CUDA-style MOG2 background model on a static background,
then applies a frame containing a bright 3x3 moving blob and reports the
foreground area of the downloaded mask. Frames and masks travel as GpuMat
values with a Stream threaded through each call, mirroring OpenCV's CUDA API.

examples.name: BackgroundSubtractorGMG
examples.doc:
ExampleBackgroundSubtractorGMG classifies the same blob with the GMG model
after its initial learning period.

examples.name: BackgroundSubtractorMOG
examples.doc:
ExampleBackgroundSubtractorMOG shows the upload/download idiom: the frame is
uploaded to a GpuMat, classified, and the mask downloaded back to host memory.

examples.name: BackgroundSubtractorMOG2
examples.doc:
ExampleBackgroundSubtractorMOG2 warms the CUDA-style MOG2 model on a static
background, then applies a frame with a bright 3×3 moving blob and reports the
foreground area. Frames and masks travel as GpuMat values and a Stream is
threaded through each call, mirroring the OpenCV CUDA API.

packages.name: cudacodec
packages.synopsis: Package cudacodec is a CPU-backed, API-compatible mirror of OpenCV's cudacodec module for the cv image toolkit (github.com/malcolmston/opencv).
packages.doc:
Package cudacodec is a CPU-backed, API-compatible mirror of OpenCV's
cudacodec module for the cv image toolkit
([github.com/malcolmston/opencv]). It reproduces the shape of OpenCV's
hardware video-codec API — [VideoReader], [VideoWriter], the [Codec],
[ColorFormat] and [SurfaceFormat] enumerations, [EncoderParams] and
[FormatInfo] — but contains no cgo, no CUDA and no GPU code whatsoever. It
builds and runs anywhere the Go toolchain does.

# What "cudacodec" means here

In upstream OpenCV, cudacodec wraps NVIDIA's NVDEC/NVENC hardware engines to
decode and encode compressed video (H.264, HEVC, VP9, AV1, …) directly on the
GPU, exchanging frames as device-resident cv::cuda::GpuMat objects. None of
that is reachable from the pure-Go standard library: the hardware codecs
require proprietary drivers and native SDKs behind cgo.

This package is therefore an honest substitution, not an emulation. It keeps
the familiar names and call shapes so code ported from OpenCV reads
naturally, while delegating all real work to the sibling
[github.com/malcolmston/opencv/videoio] package, which implements
standard-library-only containers:

  - Motion-JPEG AVI (image/jpeg inside a real RIFF/AVI container) stands in
    for the intra-coded compressed codecs ([CodecJPEG], and — by
    substitution — [CodecH264], [CodecHEVC], and friends).
  - Animated PNG / APNG (image/png) stands in for lossless streams.
  - Animated GIF (image/gif) stands in for palette-limited streams.
  - Numbered image sequences round out the set.

The concrete container is selected from the output file's extension
(".avi", ".png"/".apng", ".gif"); the [Codec] value you request is recorded
in [FormatInfo] and honoured as an intent, but the bytes on disk are always
one of the four standard-library formats above. No NVDEC/NVENC bitstream is
ever produced or consumed. See [CreateVideoWriter] and [CreateVideoReader].

# GpuMat and Stream

[GpuMat] is a thin wrapper around a host-resident [cv.Mat]; there is no device
memory, so [GpuMat.Upload] and [GpuMat.Download] are ordinary copies and
[Stream] is a no-op scheduling handle whose [Stream.WaitForCompletion] returns
immediately. They exist so that method signatures match OpenCV's
GpuMat/Stream-based API.

# Typical use

Encoding a synthetic clip and reading it back:

	w, _ := cudacodec.CreateVideoWriter("clip.avi", image.Pt(64, 48),
		cudacodec.CodecH264, 25, cudacodec.ColorFormatBGR)
	for _, m := range frames {
		_ = w.Write(cudacodec.NewGpuMatFromMat(m))
	}
	_ = w.Release()

	r, _ := cudacodec.CreateVideoReader("clip.avi")
	dst := cudacodec.NewGpuMat()
	for r.NextFrame(dst) {
		use(dst.Download())
	}
	_ = r.Release()

Because the underlying codecs are deterministic, a written clip reads back
with the same frame count and — for the lossless APNG/GIF-free paths — high
fidelity; the MJPEG path is visually faithful but lossy, exactly as a real
intra-coded codec would be.

types.name: ChromaFormat
types.signature: type ChromaFormat int
types.doc:
ChromaFormat identifies chroma subsampling, mirroring
cudacodec::ChromaFormat. It appears in [FormatInfo]; the substitution codecs
used here decode to full RGB, so it is reported as advisory metadata.

consts.doc: ChromaFormat values matching cudacodec::ChromaFormat.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the chroma format's OpenCV-style name.

types.name: Codec
types.signature: type Codec int
types.doc:
Codec identifies a video codec, mirroring the constants of OpenCV's
cudacodec::Codec enumeration. In upstream OpenCV these select an NVDEC/NVENC
hardware path; here they express intent only. The concrete on-disk container
is chosen from the output file extension (see [CreateVideoWriter]), and the
requested Codec is preserved through [FormatInfo.Codec] for round-tripping and
diagnostics. Compressed values such as [CodecH264] and [CodecHEVC] are honoured
by substitution with the Motion-JPEG AVI container.

consts.signature:
const (
	CodecMPEG1     Codec = iota // MPEG-1
	CodecMPEG2                  // MPEG-2
	CodecMPEG4                  // MPEG-4 part 2
	CodecVC1                    // SMPTE VC-1
	CodecH264                   // H.264 / AVC
	CodecJPEG                   // Motion-JPEG (the native substitution codec)
	CodecH264SVC                // H.264 SVC
	CodecH264MVC                // H.264 MVC
	CodecHEVC                   // H.265 / HEVC
	CodecVP8                    // VP8
	CodecVP9                    // VP9
	CodecAV1                    // AV1
	CodecNumCodecs              // sentinel: number of compressed codecs

	// CodecUncompressedRGBA marks a lossless, uncompressed RGBA stream. It is the
	// substitution codec reported for the APNG and GIF containers, which carry
	// full frames rather than a compressed bitstream.
	CodecUncompressedRGBA
)
consts.doc:
Codec values matching cudacodec::Codec. The ordered block mirrors OpenCV's
enumerator order so numeric values ported from OpenCV keep their meaning.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the codec's OpenCV-style name.

types.name: ColorFormat
types.signature: type ColorFormat int
types.doc:
ColorFormat identifies the pixel layout of the frames exchanged with a reader
or writer, mirroring cudacodec::ColorFormat. The cv toolkit stores frames as
interleaved 8-bit RGB or grayscale [cv.Mat] values, so a ColorFormat is
advisory metadata recorded in [FormatInfo]; no channel reordering is performed
on the pixel data.

consts.signature:
const (
	ColorFormatUndefined ColorFormat = iota // no preference
	ColorFormatBGRA                         // 4-channel BGRA
	ColorFormatBGR                          // 3-channel BGR
	ColorFormatGray                         // single-channel grayscale
	ColorFormatNV12                         // NV12 (planar YUV 4:2:0)
	ColorFormatRGB                          // 3-channel RGB
	ColorFormatRGBA                         // 4-channel RGBA
)
consts.doc: ColorFormat values matching cudacodec::ColorFormat.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the color format's OpenCV-style name.

types.name: EncoderParams
types.doc:
EncoderParams mirrors cudacodec::EncoderParams, the tuning bundle passed to a
GPU encoder. NVENC exposes dozens of rate-control knobs; this port keeps a
small, meaningful subset and applies what the standard-library codecs can
honour (notably the frame rate). The remaining fields are recorded and echoed
back so ported code that sets them still compiles and round-trips.

funcs.name: NewEncoderParams
funcs.doc:
NewEncoderParams returns EncoderParams populated with reasonable defaults: 25
fps, NV12 surface format, quality 95 and a one-second GOP.

types.name: FormatInfo
types.doc:
FormatInfo describes the format of a decoded stream, mirroring
cudacodec::FormatInfo. A reader fills it in from the container it opened; the
fields relevant to a pure-Go, CPU-decoded stream are populated and the rest
carry sensible defaults. Obtain one from [VideoReader.Format].

types.name: GpuMat
types.doc:
GpuMat mirrors OpenCV's cv::cuda::GpuMat, the device-resident matrix that the
real cudacodec API exchanges with NVDEC/NVENC. In this pure-Go port there is
no device memory: a GpuMat simply wraps a host-resident [cv.Mat]. The wrapper
exists so that method signatures match OpenCV's GpuMat-based calls
([VideoReader.NextFrame], [VideoWriter.Write]); [GpuMat.Upload] and
[GpuMat.Download] are plain host copies rather than PCIe transfers.

The zero value is an empty GpuMat ready to receive a frame; construct
instances with [NewGpuMat] or [NewGpuMatFromMat].

funcs.name: NewGpuMat
funcs.doc:
NewGpuMat returns an empty GpuMat. It holds no frame until something is
uploaded into it, so [GpuMat.Empty] reports true.

funcs.name: NewGpuMatFromMat
funcs.doc:
NewGpuMatFromMat returns a GpuMat backed by a deep copy of m, so later
mutations of m do not affect the GpuMat. A nil or empty m yields an empty
GpuMat.

methods.name: Channels
methods.doc: Channels returns the number of samples per pixel, or 0 when empty.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc: Clone returns an independent deep copy of the GpuMat.

methods.name: Cols
methods.doc: Cols returns the frame width in pixels, or 0 when empty.

methods.name: Download
methods.doc:
Download copies the GpuMat back to a host [cv.Mat], mirroring
cv::cuda::GpuMat::download. In OpenCV this is a device-to-host transfer; here
it is a deep copy, so the returned Mat is independent of the GpuMat. An empty
GpuMat downloads as nil. The variadic stream argument is ignored.

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc: Empty reports whether the GpuMat currently holds no frame.

methods.name: Mat
methods.doc:
Mat returns the GpuMat's underlying host matrix without copying, or nil when
empty. Callers that mutate the result also mutate the GpuMat; use
[GpuMat.Download] for an independent copy.

methods.name: Release
methods.doc:
Release frees the GpuMat's frame, mirroring cv::cuda::GpuMat::release. After
Release the GpuMat is empty and can be reused as an [Upload] target.

methods.name: Rows
methods.doc: Rows returns the frame height in pixels, or 0 when empty.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc:
Size returns the frame size as an image.Point in OpenCV's (width, height)
order: X is the number of columns and Y the number of rows. An empty GpuMat
reports the zero Point.

methods.name: Upload
methods.doc:
Upload copies a host [cv.Mat] into the GpuMat, mirroring
cv::cuda::GpuMat::upload. In OpenCV this is a host-to-device transfer; here it
is a deep copy, so the GpuMat is independent of m afterwards. The variadic
stream argument is accepted for API compatibility and ignored — see [Stream].
Uploading a nil or empty Mat clears the GpuMat.

types.name: Stream
types.signature: type Stream struct{}
types.doc:
Stream mirrors OpenCV's cv::cuda::Stream, the handle that queues asynchronous
GPU work. This port performs everything synchronously on the CPU, so a Stream
carries no state and every operation on it completes instantly. It exists only
so that calls taking a stream argument compile unchanged.

funcs.name: NewStream
funcs.doc:
NewStream returns a no-op [Stream]. The returned value can be passed to any
method that accepts a stream; all such work has already completed by the time
the call returns.

methods.name: QueryIfComplete
methods.doc:
QueryIfComplete reports whether all queued work has finished, mirroring
cv::cuda::Stream::queryIfComplete. It always returns true here, as work
completes synchronously.

methods.name: WaitForCompletion
methods.doc:
WaitForCompletion blocks until all queued work on the stream finishes,
mirroring cv::cuda::Stream::waitForCompletion. Because this port is
synchronous, there is never any outstanding work and the call returns
immediately.

types.name: SurfaceFormat
types.signature: type SurfaceFormat int
types.doc:
SurfaceFormat identifies the encoder input surface layout, mirroring
cudacodec::SurfaceFormat (the SF_* enumerators). As with [ColorFormat], it is
advisory metadata in this port and does not repack pixel data.

consts.signature:
const (
	SurfaceFormatUYVY SurfaceFormat = iota // packed YUV 4:2:2 (UYVY)
	SurfaceFormatYUY2                      // packed YUV 4:2:2 (YUY2)
	SurfaceFormatYV12                      // planar YUV 4:2:0 (YV12)
	SurfaceFormatNV12                      // planar YUV 4:2:0 (NV12)
	SurfaceFormatIYUV                      // planar YUV 4:2:0 (IYUV)
	SurfaceFormatBGR                       // packed 8-bit BGR
	SurfaceFormatGray                      // single-channel grayscale
)
consts.doc: SurfaceFormat values matching cudacodec::SurfaceFormat.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the surface format's OpenCV-style name.

types.name: VideoReader
types.doc:
VideoReader mirrors cudacodec::VideoReader, the GPU-accelerated decoder of the
real module. Here it decodes a standard-library container
(Motion-JPEG AVI, APNG or GIF) through the sibling
[github.com/malcolmston/opencv/videoio] package and hands frames out as
[GpuMat] values, reproducing OpenCV's grab/retrieve/nextFrame surface. Obtain
one from [CreateVideoReader] and free it with [VideoReader.Release].

funcs.name: CreateVideoReader
funcs.doc:
CreateVideoReader opens the clip at filename and returns a [VideoReader],
mirroring cudacodec::createVideoReader. The container is selected from the file
extension: ".avi" (Motion-JPEG AVI), ".png"/".apng" (APNG) and ".gif"
(animated GIF) are supported — the standard-library substitutions this port
uses in place of NVDEC hardware decoding. It returns an error for an
unsupported extension or a decode failure.

methods.name: Format
methods.doc:
Format returns the [FormatInfo] describing the open stream, mirroring
cudacodec::VideoReader::format. The values are fixed when the reader is
created and do not change as frames are consumed.

methods.name: FrameCount
methods.doc: FrameCount returns the total number of frames in the stream.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc:
Get returns the value of capture property prop, mirroring
cudacodec::VideoReader::get. The property identifiers are videoio's CAP_PROP_*
constants; position properties reflect how many frames have been consumed.

methods.name: Grab
methods.doc:
Grab advances to the next frame without decoding it into a destination,
mirroring cudacodec::VideoReader::grab. It reports whether a frame is now
available for [VideoReader.Retrieve]. The variadic stream argument is ignored.

methods.name: NextFrame
methods.doc:
NextFrame decodes the next frame into dst and reports whether a frame was
produced, mirroring cudacodec::VideoReader::nextFrame. When it returns false
the stream is exhausted and dst is left unchanged. The variadic stream
argument is accepted for API compatibility and ignored (see [Stream]). It
panics if dst is nil.

methods.name: Release
methods.doc:
Release frees the reader and its decoded frames, mirroring the destruction of
a cudacodec::VideoReader. It is safe to call more than once and always returns
nil; the error result matches OpenCV's Release shape.

methods.name: Retrieve
methods.doc:
Retrieve returns the frame selected by the most recent successful
[VideoReader.Grab], mirroring cudacodec::VideoReader::retrieve. It fills dst
and reports success; before any Grab, or once the stream is exhausted, it
returns false and leaves dst unchanged. It panics if dst is nil.

types.name: VideoWriter
types.doc:
VideoWriter mirrors cudacodec::VideoWriter, the GPU-accelerated encoder of the
real module. Here it encodes frames through the sibling
[github.com/malcolmston/opencv/videoio] package into a standard-library
container (Motion-JPEG AVI, APNG or GIF), reproducing OpenCV's write/release
surface while doing all work on the CPU. Obtain one from [CreateVideoWriter]
or [CreateVideoWriterWithParams].

funcs.name: CreateVideoWriter
funcs.signature: func CreateVideoWriter(filename string, frameSize image.Point, codec Codec, fps float64, colorFormat ColorFormat) (*VideoWriter, error)
funcs.doc:
CreateVideoWriter opens filename for writing and returns a [VideoWriter],
mirroring cudacodec::createVideoWriter. The container is chosen from the file
extension — ".avi" (Motion-JPEG AVI), ".png"/".apng" (APNG) or ".gif"
(animated GIF) — which is the standard-library substitution this port uses in
place of NVENC hardware encoding. frameSize is the intended frame size (the
writer nonetheless adopts the first written frame's actual bounds), codec is
recorded as intent, fps sets the playback rate and colorFormat is advisory
metadata. It returns an error for an unsupported extension.

funcs.name: CreateVideoWriterWithParams
funcs.signature: func CreateVideoWriterWithParams(filename string, frameSize image.Point, codec Codec, colorFormat ColorFormat, params EncoderParams) (*VideoWriter, error)
funcs.doc:
CreateVideoWriterWithParams is like [CreateVideoWriter] but takes an
[EncoderParams] bundle, mirroring the params overload of
cudacodec::createVideoWriter. The frame rate is taken from params.FPS; the
remaining fields are advisory and recorded for round-tripping.

methods.name: Codec
methods.doc:
Codec returns the codec the writer was created with, echoing the intent
recorded at construction.

methods.name: ColorFormat
methods.doc: ColorFormat returns the advisory color format the writer was created with.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc:
Get returns writer metadata by videoio CAP_PROP_* identifier: CAP_PROP_FPS,
CAP_PROP_FRAME_COUNT (frames written so far), and CAP_PROP_FRAME_WIDTH /
